tests.scenario: Onboarding introduces check-in, insights, and exercises features
steps: Install and open MindDoc as a new user
Progress through each onboarding screen without skipping
Record which features are introduced and how
tests.expected_result: At least one screen per major feature (check-in, insights, exercises) is displayed during onboarding. Each screen includes a plain-language description. No unexplained clinical terminology appears.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_1. Auto test used 'Perform the action described in FR_auto_1' which is not a verifiable step. Replaced with specific feature enumeration.

tests.scenario: Onboarding cannot be skipped before feature introductions complete
steps: Open the app as a new user
Attempt to skip or bypass onboarding immediately after the first screen
tests.expected_result: The system does not allow the user to reach the main dashboard until they have passed through all required onboarding screens, including one introduction per major feature.
tests.notes: New test not present in auto pipeline. Addresses the practical risk that users skip onboarding.

tests.scenario: Three or more educational articles on distinct mental health topics are available
steps: Log in as a free-tier user
Navigate to the educational content or resources section
Count and list the topics of available articles
tests.expected_result: At least three articles are listed, each covering a distinct mental health topic (e.g., anxiety, depression, stress). No two articles cover the same condition.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_3. Added the count requirement and the distinctness check.

tests.scenario: Educational content is readable without clinical knowledge
steps: Open any educational article available in the app
Identify any clinical or technical terms used
Check whether those terms are explained in the text
tests.expected_result: Every clinical or technical term that appears in the article is either explained inline or is a widely understood layperson's term. A non-specialist reader can understand the core message.
tests.notes: Retained from auto test with clearer verification steps.

tests.scenario: Core user flow completes without crashes
steps: Launch the app
Complete one check-in session
Navigate to the insights screen
Navigate to the settings screen
Return to the home screen
tests.expected_result: All five steps complete without a crash, force-close, or unhandled exception. Each screen loads within three seconds on a standard device.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_5. Added specific navigation path and a three-second load time threshold.

tests.scenario: App restores correctly after ten minutes in background
steps: Open MindDoc and navigate to the check-in screen
Switch to another app and leave MindDoc in the background for ten minutes
Return to MindDoc
tests.expected_result: MindDoc returns to the check-in screen within three seconds of being brought back to the foreground. No crash or blank screen occurs.
tests.notes: New test. Addresses the specific background-resume scenario specified in FR_hybrid_3.

tests.scenario: Three check-in sessions are accepted in one day and complete within three minutes
steps: Complete the first check-in session in the morning and record the start and end time
Complete the second check-in session in the afternoon
Complete the third check-in session in the evening
tests.expected_result: Each session completes in under three minutes. All three check-in records appear in mood history for the same calendar day with correct timestamps.
tests.notes: Added the three-minute constraint from FR_hybrid_4 which was absent in the auto test for this requirement.

tests.scenario: Check-in data appears in mood history within five seconds of submission
steps: Complete a check-in session
Immediately navigate to the mood history section
tests.expected_result: The just-completed check-in entry appears in the mood history list within five seconds of submission, with the correct date, time, and mood score.
tests.notes: Retained timing SLA from FR_hybrid_4.

tests.scenario: Notification arrives within ten minutes of scheduled time when app is closed
steps: Configure a reminder for a specific time that is at least fifteen minutes in the future
Force-close the app
Wait for the scheduled reminder time
tests.expected_result: A push notification from MindDoc appears on the device within ten minutes of the configured time even though the app is force-closed.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_9 to add the ten-minute tolerance and the force-close scenario. Auto test only said notification arrives 'at the scheduled time' without a testable tolerance.

tests.scenario: Tapping notification opens the check-in screen directly
steps: Receive a push notification from MindDoc
Tap the notification
tests.expected_result: The app opens and the check-in screen is immediately displayed, ready for the user to begin a session. The app does not open to the home screen first.
tests.notes: Added the 'not home screen first' negative assertion which makes the test more specific.

tests.scenario: Free-tier user completes check-in and sees mood score without subscription prompt
steps: Log in as a free-tier user
Complete a check-in session
View the results screen
tests.expected_result: A mood score and at least a one-sentence trend description are displayed on the results screen. No subscription prompt or paywall appears before or during the display of these results.
tests.notes: Revised to specify what 'basic result' means (mood score and brief trend description) per FR_hybrid_6.

tests.scenario: Premium upsell is dismissible and does not block basic result
steps: Complete a check-in as a free-tier user
Observe whether a premium subscription prompt appears
Attempt to dismiss or close the prompt
tests.expected_result: If a premium upsell appears, it includes a clearly labeled close/dismiss button. After dismissing, the basic mood summary (score and trend) remains visible.
tests.notes: New test. Addresses the specific hybrid requirement that upsells must be dismissible.

tests.scenario: Data-referenced insight appears after seven consecutive days of check-ins
steps: Complete at least one check-in per day for seven consecutive days
Navigate to the insights section
tests.expected_result: At least one insight is displayed that references a specific detected pattern with a time reference (e.g., 'Your mood has been lower on Monday mornings for the past 7 days'). The insight is not a generic tip.
tests.notes: Rewritten from T_auto_13. Added the requirement that the insight must contain a time reference and reference specific data, not generic advice.

tests.scenario: Insight content changes when mood pattern changes
steps: Log consistently high mood for seven days and note the insight shown
Log consistently low mood for the next seven days and note the new insight
tests.expected_result: The insight shown after the high-mood period differs from the insight shown after the low-mood period, demonstrating that the system updates insights based on actual data.
tests.notes: New test. Verifies the adaptive nature of insights per FR_hybrid_7.

tests.scenario: Two consecutive check-in sessions do not have identical question sets
steps: Complete a check-in session and record the questions presented
Immediately complete another check-in session and record the questions
tests.expected_result: At least one question differs between the two consecutive sessions. The sets are not completely identical.
tests.notes: Retained from auto test. Added the requirement to record and compare question sets explicitly.

tests.scenario: At least fifteen distinct questions appear across ten consecutive sessions
steps: Complete ten check-in sessions over multiple days
Record all questions presented in each session
Count the number of distinct questions seen across all ten sessions
tests.expected_result: At least fifteen unique questions have been presented across the ten sessions.
tests.notes: New test. Directly tests the ten-session / fifteen-question measurable constraint from FR_hybrid_8.

tests.scenario: Journal entry is stored and linked to the check-in record
steps: Complete a check-in and enter a unique text string in the journal field
Submit the session
Navigate to the check-in history and open the entry just completed
tests.expected_result: The journal text entered during the check-in is displayed alongside the structured mood data for that session in the history view.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_17 and T_auto_18. Combined into one clear test with a unique text string for verification.

tests.scenario: Journal entries from more than 30 days ago remain accessible
steps: Enter a journal entry during a check-in
Wait more than 30 days or simulate time passage
Navigate to the check-in history and open the old entry
tests.expected_result: The journal entry from more than 30 days ago is still displayed and complete. No data has been deleted or truncated.
tests.notes: New test. Addresses the retention requirement added in FR_hybrid_9.

tests.scenario: Weekly and monthly mood charts display accurate data
steps: Complete check-ins for at least ten days
Navigate to mood history and view the 7-day chart
Switch to the 30-day chart
tests.expected_result: Both the 7-day and 30-day charts display data for the correct time periods. The number of data points matches the number of days with completed check-ins.
tests.notes: Retained from auto tests. Merged T_auto_19 and T_auto_20 for efficiency.

tests.scenario: Mood chart updates within 30 seconds after a new check-in
steps: View the mood history chart
Complete a new check-in session
Return to the mood history chart within 30 seconds
tests.expected_result: The chart displays the new check-in data point. The update occurs within 30 seconds of check-in submission without requiring a manual refresh.
tests.notes: New test. Addresses the 30-second data freshness requirement added in FR_hybrid_10.

tests.scenario: Check-in data from more than 30 days ago is accessible
steps: Use the app for more than 30 days with regular check-ins
Navigate to mood history and scroll back to entries older than 30 days
tests.expected_result: Entries from more than 30 days ago are present and complete in the history view.
tests.notes: Retained from auto test T_auto_21.

tests.scenario: All check-in data is preserved after an app update
steps: Record several weeks of check-in data
Update the app to a newer version via the Play Store
Launch the updated app and navigate to mood history
tests.expected_result: All check-in records, mood scores, and journal entries from before the update are still present and correct. No data loss or corruption occurs during the update.
tests.notes: Retained from T_auto_22 with no changes needed.

tests.scenario: User can configure up to three notification times
steps: Open notification settings
Enable and configure three separate reminder times
Save the settings
tests.expected_result: All three configured times are saved and displayed in the settings screen as active reminders.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_23 to clarify that all three times must be shown as active after saving.

tests.scenario: Schedule change takes effect for next notification without app restart
steps: Change a reminder time to a value more than ten minutes in the future
Save without restarting the app
Wait for the new scheduled time
tests.expected_result: The notification arrives at the new configured time. The app does not need to be restarted for the change to take effect.
tests.notes: Rewritten from T_auto_24. Added the 'no restart required' negative assertion.

tests.scenario: Crisis screen appears for self-harm response and is non-dismissible without acknowledgment
steps: Start a check-in session
Select a crisis-level response indicating thoughts of self-harm
Attempt to proceed past the crisis screen by pressing back or swiping
tests.expected_result: A crisis support screen appears immediately. It contains: a supportive message, at least one crisis helpline number, and an 'I understand' or equivalent button. The screen cannot be bypassed without tapping the acknowledgment button.
tests.notes: Substantially rewritten from T_auto_25. Added the non-dismissibility test and the required content list (supportive message, helpline, acknowledgment button).

tests.scenario: Crisis helpline information is regionally appropriate
steps: Trigger a crisis response in the app
View the crisis support screen
Check the helpline number displayed
tests.expected_result: The crisis helpline number displayed corresponds to a real, active crisis line appropriate for the user's detected or configured region (e.g., 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline for US users).
tests.notes: New test. Addresses the regional relevance requirement added in FR_hybrid_13.

tests.scenario: At least 20 distinct emotion options are available in check-in
steps: Start a check-in and navigate to the emotion selection step
Count the total number of distinct labeled emotion options available
tests.expected_result: At least 20 distinct, labeled emotion options are displayed.
tests.notes: Updated threshold from 15 (T_auto_27) to 20 per FR_hybrid_14.

tests.scenario: Emotions are organized by valence with both low and high intensity options
steps: Open the emotion selection step in a check-in
Identify the organizational structure of emotion options
Check for both positive and negative valence groups
Check for both low-intensity and high-intensity options within each group
tests.expected_result: Emotions are grouped or labeled by valence (positive and negative). At least one low-intensity and one high-intensity option exists within each valence group (e.g., content vs. elated for positive; sad vs. devastated for negative).
tests.notes: New test. Addresses the valence × intensity organization requirement in FR_hybrid_14.

tests.scenario: A named coping exercise appears after a check-in reporting anxiety
steps: Complete a check-in and select anxiety as the dominant emotion
View the results or recommendations screen
tests.expected_result: At least one named coping exercise (e.g., '4-7-8 breathing,' 'progressive muscle relaxation') is displayed on the results screen, relevant to anxiety.
tests.notes: Revised from T_auto_29. Added requirement that the exercise be named, not just described generically.

tests.scenario: Recommendations differ between an anxious session and a sad session
steps: Complete a check-in reporting primary emotion as anxious
Note the coping exercise(s) recommended
Complete another check-in reporting primary emotion as sad
Note the coping exercise(s) recommended
tests.expected_result: The exercises recommended for the anxious session differ from those recommended for the sad session. At least one different exercise name appears in each recommendation.
tests.notes: Retained from T_auto_30 with minor wording refinement.
